source_status.primary_source_need: none; this slice implements repository evidence plumbing rather than a changed external fact

finding: The crash-stress workflow retained runner identity and raw logs, but did not emit the structured per-platform observation required by the deny-by-default classifier. This left manual transcription between runtime output and acceptance.
design_implication: Each matrix runner must emit a classifier-ready observation containing exact commit, platform, exit code, cycle and terminal totals, residual count, artifact paths, and SHA-256 digests. Failed tests must still retain evidence and preserve the nonzero exit status.
evidence_strength: committed implementation and deterministic test contract; runtime pending
uncertainty: No successful Linux or macOS job was available through the connector at recording time.
The builder test is committed but no passing execution is claimed.
A test failure before summary emission may prevent observation construction, though raw identity and logs remain upload candidates.
falsification_route: Run the workflow on the exact commit.
Confirm each artifact contains runner, summary, log, and observation files.
Recompute all recorded SHA-256 digests from retained bytes.
Combine Linux and macOS observations and require classifyCrashStressEvidence(...).accepted === true.
Treat any missing summary, digest mismatch, nonzero exit, wrong commit, duplicate platform, incomplete cycle count, or residual artifact as falsifying evidence.
trust_limit: A generated observation is a structured report over retained bytes. Acceptance still requires independent digest recomputation and the existing cross-platform classifier; it does not prove universal crash safety.
